novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The fiber-overlap no-test statement is the standard indistinguishable-distributions lemma and is already covered by GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026's testing-impossibility logic.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup | Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The slice \(\mathfrak P_P(G,e)\) is not defined sharply enough: if it means full bounded-outcome image, \(P\) largely drops out; if it means matching observable moments of \(P\), empty slices and distances to empty sets are not specified.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The no-interference null versus threshold-alternative witness must explicitly exclude null-equivalent/exposure-invariant alternative response surfaces; otherwise richer exposure maps can reproduce no-interference laws and force zero collision distance.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace \(\mathfrak P_P(G,e)\) with a fully specified finite object: define nonempty slices, the convention for empty null or alternative slices, and whether the distance is between model classes, local neighborhoods of \(P\), or moment-restricted fibers.

Add a primitive alternative-strength condition for Conjecture 2, such as a minimum exposure-label outcome contrast or exclusion of exposure-invariant alternatives, then re-check that the \(1/24\) lower bound is not killed by a null-equivalent threshold map.
Downgrade Theorem 1 to a supporting lemma or explicitly label it as known Le Cam/Gao fiber-overlap machinery, reserving novelty for the sparse collision modulus and noncorner witness.

recommended_next_step: Revise the fiber-slice definition and the noncorner witness restrictions before sending this to Stage 0.
